summary: I moved the dark mode toggle from the sidebar's top header row to the sidebar's footer, grouped with "Sign out" as a related pair (right-aligned via `ml-auto` in a new `flex items-center gap-2` row), per the approved technical design in `.pipeliner/phases/02-plan/main/design-writer/output/design.md`. Note: the requirements doc clarifies that "top right corner" was resolved during Define-phase clarifying questions to mean *the sidebar footer next to Sign out*, not a literal new top bar — an earlier draft approach (`technical-approach-planner`) explored a top-bar layout, but that was superseded by the requirements-driven design that already passed both the design-coverage and guide-alignment critics, so I implemented that one. Only `app/views/shared/_sidebar.html.erb` changed (header wrapper/toggle removed; footer restructured into a flex row holding "Sign out" and the toggle); the `_theme_toggle` partial, Stimulus controller, theme helper, and the auth/sign-in layout are untouched. I also extended `test/system/theme_toggle_test.rb` with two tests: one confirming the toggle now lives in the footer alongside "Sign out" (and is absent from the header), and one confirming it's still keyboard-reachable (focus + Enter toggles the theme). I couldn't execute the test suite in this sandbox — the worktree's gems aren't installed and `bundle install` fails on a permission-denied write to the global Homebrew gem path, an environment limitation unrelated to this change — but both the ERB and Ruby syntax check out cleanly.
